_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**No traditional producer/EPR registration scheme applies under LL97.** The law does not establish an extended producer responsibility (EPR) registry. Instead, it imposes a building-specific reporting obligation directly on building owners through the NYC Department of Buildings' online platform.

**Covered Buildings List (CBL):** The DOB maintains and publishes the LL97 Covered Buildings List, updated as of March 2026, which identifies buildings required to file in the current cycle. Building owners must review their entries and notify DOB of inaccuracies by submitting Ticket #1 in the BEAM Portal. The workspace should cross-check each NYC facility's Building Identification Number (BIN) against the CBL; the presence or absence of a building on the list does not relieve the owner of legal obligation.

**Annual reporting registration/filing:** Each covered building is identified by its BIN (not by tax lot/BBL). Reporting is building-by-building. The multi-step registration and filing process requires: (1) creation of a DOB NOW account and payment of the LL97 filing fee, specifying Article 320 or 321; (2) sharing of 12 months of building energy source data via EPA ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager (ESPM); (3) submission of the certified compliance report through the BEAM portal at nyc.beam-portal.org, unlocked only after DOB NOW fee payment is processed (data transfers overnight — the BEAM report cannot be completed in one day).

**Data fields the workspace must collect per covered building:**
- Building BIN and BBL (10-digit borough-block-lot number)
- Gross Floor Area (GFA) as verified by RDP, consistent with DOF records
- 12 months of utility consumption data by fuel type (electricity, natural gas, steam, fuel oil) drawn from ESPM
- Annual GHG emissions calculation (total energy use per fuel type × applicable carbon coefficient)
- Comparison of calculated emissions to the property-type-specific annual limit
- LL88 attestation data: lighting compliance and sub-meter coverage for tenant spaces >5,000 sq ft
- Supporting documentation for any deductions claimed (RECs, on-site renewables, beneficial electrification credits)

**Extension and adjustment applications:** Extension requests for the 2026 filing cycle may be submitted via BEAM by June 30, 2026 for a $60 fee (extending deadline to August 29, 2026). Adjustment applications (for excessive-emissions situations, special uses, or healthcare facilities) are submitted through DOB NOW Safety and reviewed by DOB. CBL dispute tickets (Ticket #1 in BEAM) are used to correct building records.

*Source: NYC DOB LL97 Greenhouse Gas Emissions Reduction page (March 2026). https://www.nyc.gov/site/buildings/codes/ll97-greenhouse-gas-emissions-reductions.page. NYC DOB LL97 Compliance Report Submission Process. https://www.nyc.gov/assets/buildings/pdf/ll97-compliance-report-process.pdf. NYC DOB NOW User Guide v3, April 15, 2026. https://www.nyc.gov/assets/buildings/pdf/dn_userguide.pdf. NYC Accelerator LL97 portal. https://accelerator.nyc/ll97.*
